summary: A solid matrix (mic‑board) recording delivers a good Red Rocks performance highlighted by Little Red Rooster, Tons of Steel and a thunderous Drum segment.
review: The show is praised as a strong mid‑summer ’87 outing with standout moments like Little Red Rooster, Tons of Steel, China Doll and an especially powerful Drums section. The upgraded Tobin SBD matrix source sounds clear after EQ work, though it has minor quirks such as a brief beat repeat. Overall the performance earns a respectable rating despite some set‑list unevenness.

ai_rating.rationale: The reviewer rates the show 3¾ stars and gives the recording a positive 4‑star review; combined weighting (70 % show, 30 % recording) yields an overall rating around four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tobin upgraded SBD matrix sounds clear after EQ, but has a slight repeat of beats at the start of "China Rose & Sweetheart" and occasional phasing on other versions.

standout_songs: Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets needed a warm‑up, with the second set kicking off strongly via Samson; overall solid but not as consistently fire‑filled as other ’87 shows.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Noted for “boppin’ around” early in the show, adding extra energy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s slide work is highlighted on Little Red Rooster and other tracks.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drum segment is praised as a “roll of thunder up the stalls.”
